# apeye-core

% start short_desc

**Core (offline) functionality for the apeye library.**

% end short_desc

% start shields

```
.. list-table::
        :stub-columns: 1
        :widths: 10 90

        * - Tests
          - |actions_linux| |actions_windows| |actions_macos| |coveralls|
        * - PyPI
          - |pypi-version| |supported-versions| |supported-implementations| |wheel|
        * - Anaconda
          - |conda-version| |conda-platform|
        * - Activity
          - |commits-latest| |commits-since| |maintained| |pypi-downloads|
        * - QA
          - |codefactor| |actions_flake8| |actions_mypy|
        * - Other
          - |license| |language| |requires|
```

% end shields

## Installation

% start installation

`apeye-core` can be installed from PyPI or Anaconda.

To install with `pip`:

```bash
$ python -m pip install apeye-core
```

To install with `conda`:

```bash
$ conda install -c conda-forge apeye-core
```

% end installation

modules.name: apeye_core._tld
documentation:
Functions for parsing top-level domains (TLDs).

A stripped back version of https://github.com/john-kurkowski/tldextract
!/usr/bin/env python
_tld.py

functions.name: apeye_core._tld.extract_tlds_from_suffix_list
documentation:
Parse the raw suffix list text for its different designations of suffixes.

```
param suffix_list_text



returns

A two element tuple of (public TLDs, private TLDs).
```

functions.name: apeye_core._tld.load_suffix_list
documentation:
Load the public suffix list from disk.

```
returns

A two element tuple of (public TLDs, private TLDs).
```

functions.name: apeye_core._tld.determine_suffix_index
documentation:
Returns the index of the first suffix label, or `len(elements)` if no suffix is found.

```
param tlds

The list of public TLDs.

param elements

The elements of the domain, e.g. ['forums', 'bbc', 'co', 'uk'].
```

functions.name: apeye_core._tld.looks_like_ip
documentation:
Returns whether the given string looks like an IP address.

```
param maybe_ip

```

functions.name: apeye_core._tld.extract_tld
documentation:
Takes a string URL and splits it into its subdomain, domain, and suffix
(effective TLD, gTLD, ccTLD, etc.) component.

:::{versionadded} 1.0.0 (undocumented)
:::

```python
>>> Domain._make(extract_tld('https://forums.news.cnn.com/'))
Domain(subdomain='forums.news', domain='cnn', suffix='com')
>>> Domain._make(extract_tld('https://forums.bbc.co.uk/')
Domain(subdomain='forums', domain='bbc', suffix='co.uk')
```

documentation: pylint: disable=line-too-long

modules.name: apeye_core.email_validator
documentation:
Email address validation functions.

:::{versionadded} 1.0.0
:::

This module is a subset of <https://pypi.org/project/email-validator>
!/usr/bin/env python
url.py

functions.name: apeye_core.email_validator.validate_email
documentation:
Validates an email address.

```
param email

Either a string, or ASCII-encoded bytes.

param allow_smtputf8



param allow_empty_local

Whether to allow the local part (the bit before the @-sign) to be empty.

raises EmailSyntaxError

if the address is not valid
```

functions.name: apeye_core.email_validator.validate_email_local_part
documentation:
Validates the local part of an email address (the part before the @-sign).

```
param local



param allow_smtputf8



param allow_empty_local

Whether to allow the local part to be empty/
```

functions.name: apeye_core.email_validator.validate_email_domain_part
documentation:
Validate the domain part of an email address (the part after the @-sign).

```
param domain

```

variables.name: apeye_core.email_validator.ATEXT_UTF8
documentation: Don't convert to f-string

classes.name: apeye_core.email_validator.EmailSyntaxError
documentation: Exception raised when an email address fails validation because of its form.

methods.name: apeye_core.email_validator.ValidatedEmail.__str__
documentation: Return a string representation of the {class}`~apeye.email_validator.ValidatedEmail` object.

documentation: Return a string representation of the {class}`~apeye.email_validator.ValidatedEmail` object.

documentation: Return `self == other`.

methods.name: apeye_core.email_validator.ValidatedEmail.as_dict
documentation: Convenience method for accessing the {class}`~apeye.email_validator.ValidatedEmail` as a dict.

class_variables.name: apeye_core.email_validator.ValidatedEmail.email
documentation:
The normalized email address, which should always be used in preference to the original address.

The normalized address converts an IDNA ASCII domain name to Unicode, if possible,
and performs Unicode normalization on the local part and on the domain (if originally Unicode).
It is the concatenation of the local_part and domain attributes, separated by an @-sign.

class_variables.name: apeye_core.email_validator.ValidatedEmail.smtputf8
documentation:
If {py:obj}`True`, the SMTPUTF8 feature of your mail relay will be required to transmit messages
to this address.

This flag is {py:obj}`True` when {attr}`~.ascii_local_part` is missing.
Otherwise it is {py:obj}`False`.

Represents the return type of the {func}`~apeye.email_validator.validate_email` function.

This class holds the normalized form of the email address alongside other information.

```
param original_email

The original, unnormalized email address.

param email

The normalized email address, which should always be used in preference to the original address.

param local_part

The local part of the email address after Unicode normalization.

param domain

The domain part of the email address after Unicode normalization or conversion to Unicode from IDNA ascii.

param ascii_email

If not , a form of the email address that uses 7-bit ASCII characters only.

param ascii_local_part

If not , the local part of the email address using 7-bit ASCII characters only.

param ascii_domain

If not , a form of the domain name that uses 7-bit ASCII characters only.

param smtputf8

Indicates whether SMTPUTF8 will be required to transmit messages to this address.
```

```
.. autosummary-widths:: 1/4
```

classes.name: apeye_core.URLPath

methods.name: apeye_core.URLPath.__str__
documentation: Return the string representation of the path, suitable for passing to system calls.

methods.name: apeye_core.URLPath.is_absolute
documentation:
Returns whether the path is absolute (i.e. starts with `/`).

:::{versionadded} 1.1.0  previously raised :exc:`NotImplementedError`.
:::

methods.name: apeye_core.URLPath.joinpath
documentation:
Combine this {class}`~.apeye.url.URLPath` with one or several arguments.

:::{versionadded} 1.1.0  previously raised :exc:`NotImplementedError`.
:::

```
returns

A new  representing either a subpath
(if all arguments are relative paths) or a totally different path
(if one of the arguments is absolute).
```

methods.name: apeye_core.URLPath.relative_to
documentation:
Returns the relative path to another path identified by the passed arguments.

The arguments are joined together to form a single path, and therefore the following behave identically:

```pycon
>>> URLPath("/news/sport").relative_to("/", "news")
URLPath('sport')
>>> URLPath("/news/sport").relative_to("/news")
URLPath('sport')
```

:::{versionadded} 1.1.0  previously raised :exc:`NotImplementedError`.
:::

```
param *other



raises ValueError

if the operation is not possible (because this is not a subpath of the other path)
```

```
.. latex:clearpage::
```

:::{seealso}
{meth}`~.apeye.url.URL.relative_to`, which is recommended when constructing a relative path from a {class}`~URL`.
This method cannot correctly handle some cases, such as:

```pycon
>>> URL("https://github.com/domdfcoding").path.relative_to(URL("https://github.com").path)
Traceback (most recent call last):
ValueError: '/domdfcoding' does not start with ''
```

Since `URL("https://github.com").path` is `URLPath('')`.

Instead, use:

> ```pycon
> >>> URL("https://github.com/domdfcoding").relative_to(URL("https://github.com"))
> URLPath('domdfcoding')
> ```
:::

Represents the path part of a URL.

Subclass of {class}`pathlib.PurePosixPath` that provides a subset of its methods.

:::{versionchanged} 1.1.0
Implemented {meth}`~.apeye.url.URLPath.is_absolute`, {meth}`~.apeye.url.URLPath.joinpath`,
{meth}`~.apeye.url.URLPath.relative_to`, {meth}`~.pathlib.PurePath.match`,
`anchor`, `drive`, and support for rich comparisons (`<`, `<=`, `>` and `>=`),
which previously raised {exc}`NotImplementedError`.
:::

```
.. latex:clearpage::
```

classes.name: apeye_core.URL

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.port
documentation:
The port of number of the URL as an integer, if present. Default {py:obj}`None`.

:::{versionadded} 0.7.0
:::

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.from_parts
documentation:
Construct a {class}`~apeye.url.URL` from a scheme, netloc and path.

```
param scheme

The scheme of the URL, e.g 'http'.

param netloc

The netloc of the URl, e.g. 'bbc.co.uk:80'.

param path

The path of the URL, e.g. '/news'.

param query

The query parameters of the URL, if present.

param fragment

The URL fragment, used to identify a part of the document.
 if absent from the URL.
```

Put together, the resulting path would be `'http://bbc.co.uk:80/news'`

```
rtype

```

:::{versionchanged} 0.7.0  Added the ``query`` and ``fragment`` arguments.
:::

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.__str__
documentation: Returns the {class}`~apeye.url.URL` as a string.

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.__repr__
documentation: Returns the string representation of the {class}`~apeye.url.URL`.

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.__truediv__
documentation:
Construct a new {class}`~apeye.url.URL` object for the given child of this {class}`~apeye.url.URL`.

```
rtype

```

:::{versionchanged} 0.7.0
- Added support for division by integers.
- Now officially supports the new path having a URL fragment and/or query parameters.
  Any URL fragment or query parameters from the parent URL are not inherited by its children.
:::

methods.name: apeye_core.URL._make_child
documentation:
Construct a new {class}`~apeye.url.URL` object by combining the given arguments with this instance's path part.

:::{versionadded} 1.1.0  (private)
:::

Except for the final path element any queries and fragments are ignored.

```
returns

A new  representing either a subpath
(if all arguments are relative paths) or a totally different path
(if one of the arguments is absolute).
```

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.joinurl
documentation:
Construct a new {class}`~apeye.url.URL` object by combining the given arguments with this instance's path part.

:::{versionadded} 1.1.0
:::

Except for the final path element any queries and fragments are ignored.

```
returns

A new  representing either a subpath
(if all arguments are relative paths) or a totally different path

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.__fspath__
documentation:
Returns the file system path representation of the {class}`~.apeye.url.URL`.

This is comprised of the `netloc` and `path` attributes.

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.__eq__
documentation:
Return `self == other`.

```
.. latex:vspace:: -10px
```

::::{attention}
URL fragments and query parameters are not compared.

:::{seealso}
{meth}`.URL.strict_compare`, which *does* consider those attributes.
:::
::::

```
.. latex:vspace:: -20px
```

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.strict_compare
documentation:
Return `self ≡ other`, comparing the scheme, netloc, path, fragment and query parameters.

:::{versionadded} 0.7.0
:::

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.__hash__
documentation: Returns the has of the {class}`~apeye.url.URL` .

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.name
documentation: The final path component, if any.

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.suffix
documentation:
The final component's last suffix, if any.

This includes the leading period. For example: `'.txt'`.

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.suffixes
documentation:
A list of the final component's suffixes, if any.

These include the leading periods. For example: `['.tar', '.gz']`.

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.stem
documentation: The final path component, minus its last suffix.

and fragment from this .

rtype

```

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.with_suffix
documentation:
Returns a new {class}`~apeye.url.URL` with the file suffix changed.

If the {class}`~apeye.url.URL` has no suffix, add the given suffix.

If the given suffix is an empty string, remove the suffix from the {class}`~apeye.url.URL`.

```
param suffix



param inherit

Whether the new  should inherit the query string
and fragment from this .

rtype

```

:::{versionchanged} 0.7.0  Added the ``inherit`` parameter.
:::

methods.name: apeye_core.URL.parts
documentation:
An object providing sequence-like access to the components in the URL.

To retrieve only the parts of the path, use {meth}`URL.path.parts <URLPath.parts>`.

methods.name: apeye_core.URL._parts_port
documentation:
An object providing sequence-like access to the components in the URL.

Unlike `.parts` this includes the port.

To retrieve only the parts of the path, use {meth}`URL.path.parts <URLPath.parts>`.

:::{versionadded} 1.1.0  (private)
:::

classes.name: apeye_core.Domain

methods.name: apeye_core.Domain.registered_domain
documentation:
Joins the domain and suffix fields with a dot, if they're both set.

```python
>>> URL('https://forums.bbc.co.uk').domain.registered_domain
'bbc.co.uk'
>>> URL('https://localhost:8080').domain.registered_domain
''
```

methods.name: apeye_core.Domain.fqdn
documentation:
Returns a Fully Qualified Domain Name, if there is a proper domain/suffix.

```python
>>> URL('https://forums.bbc.co.uk/path/to/file').domain.fqdn
'forums.bbc.co.uk'
>>> URL('https://localhost:8080').domain.fqdn
''
```

methods.name: apeye_core.Domain.ipv4
documentation:
Returns the ipv4 if that is what the presented domain/url is.

```python
>>> URL('https://127.0.0.1/path/to/file').domain.ipv4
IPv4Address('127.0.0.1')
>>> URL('https://127.0.0.1.1/path/to/file').domain.ipv4
>>> URL('https://256.1.1.1').domain.ipv4
```

methods.name: apeye_core.Domain.__repr__
documentation: Return a string representation of the {class}`~.Domain`.
{class}`typing.NamedTuple` of a URL's subdomain, domain, and suffix.
